description: You are the system administrator in charge of preparing per–user configuration files that mimic how a real Linux server would handle its time-zone and locale settings. Because you do **not** have root privileges inside this training environment, the task is to implement the whole set-up completely under the home directory (/home/user) while still following the same layout and naming conventions that real system files use. Objective --------- 1. Build a small directory tree that cleanly separates time-zone data from locale data. 2. Write two plain-text configuration files that contain the actual settings. 3. Provide an executable shell script that applies those settings in *parallel* (two background jobs that run at the same time, followed by a wait). 4. Produce a single log file whose exact three-line format the automated test will verify. Directory layout that **must** exist at the end ------------------------------------------------ /home/user/server_configs/ <- main root created by you ├── time_settings/ <- all time-zone related items │ └── timezone.txt <- contains one line with the desired TZ string ├── locale_settings/ <- all locale related items │ ├── locale.gen <- list of generated locales (one line) │ └── locale.conf <- key=value pairs exactly like systemd’s file └── apply_settings.sh <- shell script you write └── apply_settings.log <- log produced when the script is executed Exact contents the grader will look for --------------------------------------- 1. /home/user/server_configs/time_settings/timezone.txt • File must contain **exactly**: Europe/Berlin (with a single trailing newline). 2. /home/user/server_configs/locale_settings/locale.gen • File must contain **exactly**: en_US.UTF-8 UTF-8 (with a single trailing newline). 3. /home/user/server_configs/locale_settings/locale.conf • File must contain the following five separate lines in this order (each terminated by a newline): LANG=en_US.UTF-8 LC_CTYPE=en_US.UTF-8 LC_TIME=de_DE.UTF-8 LC_NUMERIC=en_US.UTF-8 LC_MONETARY=en_US.UTF-8 4. /home/user/server_configs/apply_settings.sh • Must be executable (chmod +x). • When executed from any location via bash /home/user/server_configs/apply_settings.sh it must: a) Spawn Job #1 in the background to copy timezone.txt to /home/user/.timezone. b) Spawn Job #2 in the background to append the five locale variables (exactly as found in locale.conf) to /home/user/.bashrc *only if they are not already present*. c) Use a solitary wait command to hold until both background jobs finish. d) Echo the single line “Timezone and locale applied.” to STDOUT. • The script must use “&” (ampersand) or & in two separate lines to create the two background jobs in step a and b. 5. /home/user/server_configs/apply_settings.log • Must be generated **by the script itself** (e.g. using redirection or tee). • After the script is executed once, the log **must contain exactly three lines** in this rigid format (no extra spaces, uppercase/lowercase must match exactly): [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] Copied timezone to /home/user/.timezone [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] Updated /home/user/.bashrc with locale settings [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] Settings applied successfully where • [ and ] are literal characters, • YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS is the local time string produced by date "+%F %T", and • each entry ends with a newline and nothing else. Important behavioural notes --------------------------- • The script must be idempotent: running it a second time should not duplicate locale lines in .bashrc, and the timestamps in apply_settings.log should continue to grow by **exactly three more lines per run** (the test will only look at the first three for verification). • All operations must be done without sudo. • You may create intermediate directories or files as long as they remain under /home/user, but only the items listed above are essential for grading. Deliverables you must produce ----------------------------- 1. The three configuration files with the exact contents specified. 2. The executable script implementing the parallel logic. 3. The first run of that script so that apply_settings.log exists and matches the required three-line pattern. Remember: the grader will check for the exact path names, file permissions, script behaviour and the strict log format, so double-check everything before you finish.
truth:
The automated verification suite will perform the following checks:

1. Paths:
   - /home/user/server_configs exists and is a directory.
   - /home/user/server_configs/time_settings/timezone.txt exists.

2. Content checks (exact match as described):
   - timezone.txt must be the single line: "Europe/Berlin\n"
   - locale.gen must be the single line: "en_US.UTF-8 UTF-8\n"
   - locale.conf must be exactly 5 lines as specified.

3. Script checks:
   - /home/user/server_configs/apply_settings.sh must have executable bit set (0700, 0750, 0755 or similar).
   - The script must contain at least two separate lines that end with "&" before the wait command.
   - The script must reference both ".timezone" and ".bashrc".

4. After running the script (grader will run it once in a subshell):
   - /home/user/.timezone must exist and contain "Europe/Berlin\n".
   - The five locale variables must now be present exactly once each somewhere inside /home/user/.bashrc.
   - /home/user/server_configs/apply_settings.log must exist and contain exactly 3 lines.
   - Each of the three lines must match the regex:
     ^\[\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\] (Copied timezone to /home/user/\.timezone|Updated /home/user/\.bashrc with locale settings|Settings applied successfully)$
   - The first log line contains the "Copied timezone…" text, second "Updated …", third "Settings applied…".

The grader will reject if any additional lines are present before those first three lines or if the sequence/order deviates.
